evals.prompt: I have an assessment of my project's architecture in ASSESSMENT.md. It recommends splitting a god module into focused modules. Please execute the refactoring.
evals.expected_output: A chunked refactoring plan in refactor-plan.md, a manifest in refactor-manifest.json, the god module split into auth.js/users.js/data.js/email.js, all imports updated, and all 17 tests still passing.

evals.prompt: I started a refactoring yesterday but didn't finish. The manifest and plan are already in the project. Pick up where I left off and continue the refactoring.
evals.expected_output: Agent reads the manifest, identifies the next pending chunk, and continues executing from that point without re-doing completed work. All tests pass after each chunk.

evals.prompt: I have an assessment of my project's architecture in ASSESSMENT.md. Execute the refactoring. Note: the test for handleUploadCSV is sensitive to import paths — if you change how data.js is imported in api.js, the test may break.
evals.expected_output: When a chunk causes test failures, the agent stops, logs the failure, and asks the user how to proceed rather than silently working around it or continuing to the next chunk.
